slides.title: Cold rice beats *fresh-cooked rice*.
slides.sub: Cooling cooked starches creates resistant starch — a fiber that feeds your gut and steadies your energy.

slides.title: Cooling rewrites the starch
slides.body: Once cooked starch cools, part of it becomes *resistant starch* — your gut digests it completely differently.

slides.title: Fuel for good bacteria
slides.body: Resistant starch feeds gut microbes that make *butyrate* — a compound linked to a calmer, healthier gut lining.

slides.title: A gentler glucose curve
slides.body: Reheated, cooled rice can blunt the *blood-sugar spike* versus the same food eaten fresh and hot.

slides.title: Cook, chill, reheat
slides.body: Make rice, potatoes, or pasta, chill *overnight in the fridge*, then reheat — the resistant starch sticks around.

slides.title: Build it into meals
slides.body: Cold potato salad, sushi rice, next-day pasta. *Yesterday's carbs* quietly work in your favor.

slides.title: Eat smarter, *one meal ahead*.
slides.body: Snap your meals and Vibe Health logs your food, water, sleep, and steps into one daily Health Score — so small swaps like this add up.

caption:
Cooling cooked rice, potatoes, and pasta creates resistant starch — and your gut treats it completely differently. 🍚

It feeds the bacteria that make butyrate and can blunt the blood-sugar spike when you reheat it. Cook, chill overnight, reheat. Save this. 🔖

Snap your meals and Vibe Health turns your food, sleep, and steps into one daily Health Score → link in bio.
